title: Xenial Light Norwegian Natto Stew
description: A briny and light Norwegian post-workout stew featuring natto, cooked in a clay pot, suitable for a satvik diet. This dish is easy to prepare and packed with nutrients.
instructions: Preheat the clay pot by filling it with water and letting it sit for 30 minutes before cooking.
Cut the Norwegian salmon fillet into small pieces and set aside.
Add the kombu seaweed sheets to the clay pot and pour in the filtered water.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and add the sliced carrots and cubed potatoes.
Let the carrots and potatoes cook for 10 minutes, or until they are slightly tender.
Add the chopped onion and celery to the clay pot and continue to simmer for another 5 minutes.
Stir in the natto and cook for an additional 2 minutes, or until it is well combined with the stew.
Add the Norwegian salmon pieces to the clay pot and cook until they are cooked through, about 5 minutes.
Season the stew with coarse Norwegian sea salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste.
Serve the Xenial Light Norwegian Natto Stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
